title: crataegus macracantha var. integriloba
intro: Crataegus macracantha var. integriloba, also known as hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its thorny branches and showy white flowers. The medicinal properties of hawthorn are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular-protective effects. The health benefits of Crataegus macracantha var. integriloba include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular function, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Hawthorn can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, tinctures, and capsules, often used in traditional medicine to promote cardiovascular health and overall well-being.
